2004-01-01.title: An Odd Sight in Iran as Aid Team Tents Go Up: The U.S. Flag
2004-01-01.abstract: Some Iranians are suspicious at sight of US flag being used in official capacity to mark tents housing American relief team after earthquake in Bam; team expects to stay one week, and will work with Iranian Red Crescent to help assess damage to city's infrastructure and draw up list of needs to be distributed to international donors (M)

2004-01-01.title: Iranians Appear to Warm to Easing Tensions With U.S.
2004-01-01.abstract: American aid to Iran, granted after the devastating earthquake in the city of Bam, could bring an improvement to the two countries long-frigid relationship.

2004-01-01.title: New Math Adds Up to an Extended Run for Ukraine's President
2004-01-01.abstract: Ukrainian Supreme Court rules that Pres Leonid Kuchma is exempt from Constitution's two-term limit because Constitution itself has only been in place for eight years; opposition party condemns ruling (M)

2004-01-01.title: No 'Auld Lang Syne' for Jailed Russian Oilman
2004-01-01.abstract: Prison warden says Russian oil billionaire Mikhail B Khodorkovsky will be allowed to usher in New Year by watching television, but will not be permitted to sing or drink champagne (M)

2004-01-01.title: London Journal; The Sceptered Isle Is Appraised: $8 Trillion and Change
2004-01-01.abstract: United Kingdom, including England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Island, is officially valued at $8.8 trillion; statisticians arrived at figure using new models and more detailed information measuring all private and public assets; some Britons are appalled at expenditure to arrive at valuation; photos (M)

2004-01-02.title: Egyptian Urges Arafat to Subdue Factions
2004-01-02.abstract: Egyptian government envoy Osama el-Baz meets with embattled Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat in Ramallah; carries letter to Arafat from Pres Hosni Mubarak, who is trying to arrange new meeting of Palestinian faction leaders in hopes of getting them to agree to stop attacks against Israelis; Egyptian-sponsored meeting of dozen Palestinian leaders in Cairo early last month ended in stalemate; Arafat, standing at Baz's side, says little to reporters that would indicate any new initiative is under way; photo (M)

2004-01-02.title: In Iraq's Murky Battle, Snipers Offer U.S. a Precision Weapon
2004-01-02.abstract: United States Army in Iraq is increasingly relying on its snipers to protect infantry patrols sweeping through urban streets and alleyways and to kill guerrilla leaders and disrupt their attacks; commanders say snipers are different breed of warrior--quiet, unflappable marksmen who bring dispassionate intensity to their deadly task; demand for snipers is great enough that Army has sent team of trainers to Iraq to keep churning out new ones for war effort there and in other hot spots; sniper school is placing increasing emphasis on urban tactics as Army faces more conflicts like those in Iraq in which terrorists use tight confines of city blocks and rooftops to stage hit-and-run strikes; photo (M)

2004-01-02.title: Killed in Iraq
2004-01-02.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of 480th service member in Iraq since start of Iraq war (S)

2004-01-02.title: Bush Says Iran Must Do More Before Relations Can Improve
2004-01-02.abstract: Pres Bush says he welcomes signs of warmer dealings with Iran in recent weeks, but that country must still rid itself of terrorists and nuclear weapons programs and open its political system before he will be ready to improve relations with Iran; direct talks with Iran were suspended in May after series of bombings in Saudi Arabia were traced by American intelligence officials to groups operating in Iran; warm welcome received by American relief workers arriving in earthquake-stricken city of Bam had raised possibility of end to freeze that has lasted since Iranian hostage crisis nearly quarter century ago (M)

2004-01-02.title: British Flight Called Off After Security Concerns
2004-01-02.abstract: British Airways canceled a mid-afternoon flight from London to Washington today for the second day in a row.

2004-01-03.title: A Top Khmer Rouge Leader, Going Public, Pleads Ignorance
2004-01-03.abstract: Cambodia moves closer to convening trial for deaths of 1.7 million people under brutal rule of Khmer Rouge in 1970's; Khieu Samphan, one of top Khmer Rouge leaders, has begun to plead his case publicly, claiming ignorance, innocence, shock and contrition (M)

2004-01-03.title: Private Group Prepares Visit To North Korea
2004-01-03.abstract: Private delegation of US experts on North Korea, including former National Security Council staff member Jack Pritchard and former Los Alamos National Laboratory dir Sigfried Hecker, will travel to North Korea and possibly visit nuclear weapons plant; Bush administration officials say delegation does not have official government blessing; express concern that trip might complicate administration's own delicate diplomacy with North Korea; John W Lewis, professor emeritus of international relati ons at Stanford University, will lead delegation (M)

2004-01-03.title: Kurdish Islamic Leader Arrested in Norway on Murder Charges
2004-01-03.abstract: Norwegian police arrest Mullah Krekar, spiritual leader of Islamic militant group Ansar al-Islam, on charges of attempted murder in connection with two suicide bombings in northern Iraq in 2002 (M)

2004-01-03.title: To Help Poor Be Pupils, Not Wage Earners, Brazil Pays Parents
2004-01-03.abstract: Brazil is giving poor families small cash payments if they keep their children in school and take them for regular medical checkups; Pres Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva is consolidating array of cash transfer programs, expanding his version of model, named Family Grant, and tripling average monthly benefit, to about $24; Family Grant will reach 11.4 million families by 2006--over 45 million people, about quarter of Brazil's population; that would be world's largest such program; W orld Bank and Inter-American Development Bank are putting up $3 billion in loans for program; Nicaragua, Honduras and Mexico have adopted similar programs; photos (M)

2004-01-03.title: IRAN TURNS DOWN AMERICAN OFFER OF RELIEF MISSION
2004-01-03.abstract: Bush administration says Iran has rebuffed offer from US to send delegation led by Sen Elizabeth Dole to assist in distribution of relief supplies to earthquake victims in Bam; says Iran cited difficulties facing relief workers in ancient city, but did not rule out possibility of future visit; expresses concern for Iran at time when US has declared that some of Tehran's recent actions have been positive and could lead to resumption of dialogue to improve relations; photo of Muslims praying in damaged mosque (M)

2004-01-04.title: India and Pakistan Offer Hint of Thaw
2004-01-04.abstract: Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee of India stepped onto Pakistani soil for the first time in four years, his mere presence feeding the hopes for peace between India and Pakistan.

2004-01-04.title: Egyptian Charter Jet Crashes in Red Sea, Killing 148
2004-01-04.abstract: Flash Airlines Boeing 737 en route to Paris crashes shortly after take-off from Sharm el-Sheik, Egypt, killing all 148 people on board, mostly French, Japanese and Moroccan tourists, plus Egyptian crew; crash is tentatively blamed on technical failure, rather than terrorism, though flight recorder has not been recovered; this is third crash of Egyptian commercial airliner in four years; photo, maps (M)

2004-01-04.title: Indian Leader's Trip to Pakistan Raises Hope for Warmer Ties
2004-01-04.abstract: Indian Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee visits Pakistan for meeting of South Asian Assn for Regional Cooperation; discussions do not include fate of Kashmir, over which India and Pakistan have repeatedly fought, but visit is seen as tentative step toward improving relations, which hardliners on neither side welcome; there has been 'pent-up demand for people-to-people contact' since Vajpayee extended 'hand of friendship' to Pakistan seven months ago; photo (M)

2004-01-04.title: Parmalat Stake to Be Sold
2004-01-04.abstract: Parmalat founder Calisto Tanzi, in jail on fraud charges, will surrender his stake in insolvent company and some of his personal assets to company's administrator; Tanzi family holds about 50 percent of company; Tanzi does not say whether his offer includes entire holding (S)

2004-01-04.title: Israelis Kill 4 Palestinians During Operation in Nablus
2004-01-04.abstract: Israeli Army kills four young Palestinians in Nablus, including one during funeral of the others; shootings, including that at funeral procession, were in response to rock throwing and sightings of firebombs or handguns among Palestinian youths and came as Israeli incursion into city enters its second week; photo (M)

2004-01-05.title: Kurdish Region in Northern Iraq Will Get to Keep Special Status
2004-01-05.abstract: Bush administration decides to let Kurdish region remain semiautonomous as part of newly sovereign Iraq, despite warnings from Iraq's neighbors and many Iraqis not to divide country into ethnic states; American and Iraqi officials say position on Kurdish area was effectively dictated by Nov 15 accord with Iraqi leaders that established June 30 as target date for Iraqi self-rule; say rapid timetable leaves no time to change autonomy and unity of Kurdish stronghold of north; administration and Iraq officials insist that leaving Kurdish autonomous region intact does not preclude country's consolidating itself without ethnic states in future when Iraq writes its own constitution; say Bush administration will press Iraq not to divide itself permanently along ethic lines; issue of whether Iraq is to be divided into ethnic states in federation-style government is of great significance both inside country and throughout Middle East, where there is widespread fear that dividing Iraq along ethnic or sectarian lines could break country up and spread turmoil throughout region; photo (M)

2004-01-05.title: Colombians, With U.S. Aid, Tracked Rebel For Months
2004-01-05.abstract: Colombian agents, working with American intelligence operatives, tracked top Colombian guerrilla commander Ricardo Palmera for months before he was arrested on busy street in Quito, Ecuador; Palmera was leading negotiator in peace talks that failed in 2002; Colombian government is calling his capture a major blow against Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia, Marxist rebel group, and proof that Pres Alvaro Uribe's efforts to weaken rebels is working; photo (M)

2004-01-05.title: Fear of a Communist Rebellion Is Growing in the Philippines
2004-01-05.abstract: Communist rebellion in Philippines that began 35 years ago foundered but has regained strength; military estimates there are 10,000 fighters in its armed wing, New People's Army; Philippine military spokesman says Communists are 'utmost security concern' even though they have been overshadowed by Muslim insurgents; says they are much bigger threat than Abu Sayyaf, Moro Islamic Liberation Front or Jemaah Islamiyah (M)

2004-01-05.title: AFGHAN COUNCIL GIVES APPROVAL TO CONSTITUTION
2004-01-05.abstract: New Constitution for Afghanistan is approved by acclamation by 502 delegates at national meeting, concluding three weeks of often tense debate; for first time, Afghans have set up democratic presidential system, with directly elected president and two-chamber national assembly; elections are to be held in just six months; independent judiciary is also being organized; country will be renamed Islamic Republic of Afghanistan, combining democracy and religion; there is to be system of civil law, but no law will be contrary to beliefs and provisions of Islam; new bedrock of government is welcomed by Afghan human rights and women's activists as offering prospect of rule of law in ravaged country; diplomats and foreign experts who observed process praise it for being coherent and even forward looking for region; Pres Bush welcomes new Constitution; Americans hope new arrangements will provide stronger government that can help Afghanistan rebuild after war that defeated Taliban government that had befriended Osama bin Laden; photos; chart with highlights of Afghan pact (L)

2004-01-05.title: Risks Seen in Chinas Plan to Slaughter Cats to Fight SARS
2004-01-05.abstract: W.H.O. officials today warned that Chinas slaughter, if done improperly, risked the possibility of more infections.

2004-01-06.title: Kabul Journal; With Future Charted, U.N. Envoy Departs
2004-01-06.abstract: United Nations special representative Lakhdar Brahimi, one of main architects of Afghanistan's new Constitution, prepares to bow out after two years in job; Brahimi, an Algerian diplomat, has been loyal ally to Afghans since brokering Bonn accords in Dec 2001 for new Afghan order after defeat of Taliban; is personal friend of Pres Hamid Karzai, and has served in Afghanistan twice; was UN special envoy from 1997 to 1999, during Taliban years; photo (M)

2004-01-06.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-06.abstract: GEORGIA: Landslide Expected; NEW NATO CHIEF TAKES OVER; FRANCE: Muslims Lash Out At Planned Head Scarf Ban; NORWAY: Islamic Militant Ordered Freed;.

2004-01-06.title: India and Pakistan Plan Talks Aiming to Settle Kashmir Dispute
2004-01-06.abstract: The two countries, which have fought three wars and two years ago nearly fought a fourth, today heralded a new chapter in their relationship.

2004-01-06.title: Army Delays Discharge for Some G.I.'s in Afghanistan and Mideast
2004-01-06.abstract: United States Army is preparing order that would require about 7,000 troops now in Iraq, Kuwait and Afghanistan to remain on duty through end of their deployments this spring, even if they could otherwise leave service before then; another order, previously announced, already prevents active-duty and reserve troops rotating into Iraq and Kuwait this year from leaving Army before serving 12 months on ground, plus 90-day period after they return (M)

2004-01-06.title: British Aide Sees Long Iraq Stay
2004-01-06.abstract: British Foreign Secretary Jack Straw says British troops will stay in Iraq for years, perhaps until 2007, to help restore country's security and stability (S)

2004-01-07.title: Israeli Officials Report Secret Meeting With Libyans
2004-01-07.abstract: Israeli and Libyan officials held a secret meeting in December and discussed the possibility of ties between the longstanding enemies, Israeli officials said today.

2004-01-07.title: INDIANS IN DEAL WITH PAKISTANIS FOR PEACE TALKS
2004-01-07.abstract: India and Pakistan agree to restart formal peace talks on all issues, including Kashmir; Pakistan says it will not permit its territory to be used to support terrorism, first such direct assurance to India in more than half century of conflict; agreement follows Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee's meeting with Pakistani Pres Pervez Musharraf and other less publicized negotiations, some during regional summit meeting in Islamabad; photos, one of Vajpayee with Pakistani Prime Min Mir Zafarullah Jamali; map; Musharraf says 'history has been made'; Vajpayee, 79, offers 'hand of friendship' in hopes of leaving legacy of peace; Musharraf's promise to crack down on militants whom Pakistan has long supported, and who are linked to two recent assassination attempts on him, appears to complete U-turn he made after Sept 11, 2001, when he abandoned Pakistan's support for Taliban in Afghanistan; Pakistan in turn wins long-sought dialogue on Kashmir, where it demands right of self-determination by mostly Muslim population (M)

2004-01-07.title: Bechtel Wins Its Second Big Contract for Iraq
2004-01-07.abstract: Bechtel National wins contract that could be worth $1.82 billion over two years, to help restore power, water and other essential services in Iraq (S)

2004-01-07.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Coke And Pepsi To Pull Soda From Schools
2004-01-07.abstract: Canadian soft drink manufacturers will discontinue distribution of soft drinks, including Coca-Cola and Pepsi, for vending machines in Canadian elementary and junior high schools; pressure from politicians, educators and parents to present healthier choices for children precipitated change; high schools will still offer soft drinks (S)

2004-01-07.title: In Cambodia, an Anniversary Renews Call for Genocide Trials
2004-01-07.abstract: Twenty five years after the end of the murderous Khmer Rouge regime, a top Cambodian government official said today that the time had come to put surviving leaders on trial.

2004-01-08.title: Scientists Predict Widespread Extinction by Global Warming
2004-01-08.abstract: International group of 19 scientists, analyzing research around globe, warns that warming climate will rival habitat destruction in causing widespread species extinctions this century; predict that 15-37 percent of 1,013 species studied will be doomed by 2050 if current warming trends continue; Drs Chris D Thomas and Daniel B Botkin comment on report in journal Nature (M)

2004-01-08.title: Greek Prime Minister Says He Will Step Down After Election
2004-01-08.abstract: Greek Prime Min Costas Simitis will call parliamentary election in March, month early, and resign next day, after eight years of leading ruling party (M)

2004-01-08.title: Sense of Mortality Gave Push to India-Pakistan Talks
2004-01-08.abstract: Pakistan's decision to end state support for Islamic militants in Kashmir followed eight months of secret talks and assassination attempt that seems to have confirmed Gen Pervez Musharraf's decision; photo (M)

2004-01-08.title: Qaddafi's Surprise: Why a New Stance?
2004-01-08.abstract: Something seems to have come over one of the world's best-known boogeymen, Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi, who after making Libya a pariah in the 1980's, pledged last month to end its illicit arms programs.

2004-01-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: Hong Kong: Democracy Side Step
2004-01-08.abstract: Donald Tsang, Hong Kong's second highest government official, reports that Chinese President Hu Jintao has expressed concern over proposed democratic changes for Hong Kong and has conveyed doubts to Chief Executive Tung Chee-hwa; officials in Beijing have requested further discussion and Hong Kong committee has been appointed to meet with minland government (S)

2004-01-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Students Chase Schröder From Building
2004-01-09.abstract: Hundreds of students, who are protesting proposed cuts in university financing, take over building in Leipzig (Germany) where Chancellor Gerhard Schroder is scheduled to sign official application for city to hold 2012 Olympic Games; signing ceremony is moved (S)

2004-01-09.title: At Least 4 Killed in Bomb Blast at Mosque in Iraq
2004-01-09.abstract: The attack also wounded dozens and was a further indication of the depth of religious and political animosity in Iraq.

2004-01-09.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-09.abstract: GREECE: Foreign Minister Seeks LeaderS Post; GERMANY: Students Chase SchrÖDer From Building; GEORGIA: Region Imposes Emergency; BRITAIN: Tuition Raises Proposed; BRITAIN: Viking Hopes Cruelly Dashed;.

2004-01-09.title: Mexico City Journal; Of Gringos and Old Grudges: This Land Is Their Land
2004-01-09.abstract: Almost no one in United States remembers war that Americans fought against Mexico more than 150 years ago; in Mexico, almost no one has forgotten; war cut Mexico in two and, according to Mexico City historian Miguel Soto, inflicted wound that never really healed; as Pres Bush prepares to fly down to Mexico from Texas, where war began in 1846, debate in Mexico City is over how to relate to US; photo (M)

2004-01-09.title: Fabled Trove of Fabergé Eggs Goes to Auction, Jewels and All
2004-01-09.abstract: Sotheby's in New York will auction nine Imperial Faberge Easter eggs from Czarist Russia that were acquired over lifetime by late publishing magnate Malcolm Forbes; collection is second in size only to 10 in Kremlin; it will be offered in April along with 180 other Faberge objects from Forbes holdings; together they are expected to sell for at least $90 million; growing number of newly rich Russian collectors have added to strength of this market; another potential buyer is Kremlin, which might want to add to its trove of Imperial eggs; extraordinary materials and workmanship that went into eggs, coupled with their size (six inches of less) make them perhaps most luxurious, over-the-top baubles ever produced; Malcolm Forbes' son Christopher says he and his siblings have been receiving offers from collectors all over world willing to pay handsomely for their eggs; photo (M)

2004-01-10.title: Swiss Hold 8 Suspects in Saudi Attacks
2004-01-10.abstract: Swiss police arrest eight foreign nationals suspected of involvement in suicide bomb attacks in May 2003 in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, that killed 35 people (S)

2004-01-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: Hong Kong: Harbor Reprieve
2004-01-10.abstract: Hong Kong Court of Final Appeal blocks government plans to fill in section of Victoria Harbor for proposed covered highway; requires government show overriding public need to carry out plan (S)

2004-01-10.title: U.S. and Brazil Fingerprinting: Is It Getting Out of Hand?
2004-01-10.abstract: Brazil and US are holding fast to their insistence on photographing and fingerprinting visitors from other country; what began as minor spat is threatening to sour relations between two countries; dispute grew out of new US security program that applies to all foreigners entering country who are required to have visas; US has identified border region where Brazil, Argentina and Paraguay come together as haven for Islamic terrorists (M)

2004-01-10.title: Hussein Given P.O.W. Status; Access Sought
2004-01-10.abstract: Defense Dept designates Saddam Hussein as prisoner of war, legal status that sets standards for how he is treated and allows International Committee of Red Cross to see him; information about Hussein has been tightly guarded, although members of Iraqi Governing Council were allowed to confirm his identity; senior US officials say FBI has become increasingly involved in questioning Hussein, signaling that authorities view him as potential defendant in legal proceeding as well as intelligence source; CIA remains in overall control, but FBI involvement reflects CIA reluctance to allow covert officers to take part in interrogations that could force them to appear as court witnesses (M)

2004-01-10.title: Suicide Bomber Outside Shiite Mosque in Iraq Kills Four Worshipers
2004-01-10.abstract: Suicide bomber blows himself up outside Shiite mosque in Baquba, Iraq, killing 4 other people and wounding 37; photos (M)

2004-01-11.title: 7 Held in South Korean Scandal
2004-01-11.abstract: Six South Korean politicians of different parties and SK Group chmn Son Kil Seung are arrested on charges in connection with widening inquiry into illegal campaign contributions and bribery; arrests are troubling to President Roh Moo Hyun, who entered office on pledge to fight corruption (M)

2004-01-11.title: 3rd Suspected SARS Case Emerges in China Province
2004-01-11.abstract: Health officials expressed growing worry over the weekend about the possibility of another outbreak Chinas Guangdong Province.

2004-01-11.title: Survivor of Somalia and Cancer Dies on Doomed Copter in Iraq
2004-01-11.abstract: Chief Warrant Officer Aaron A Weaver, 32, who survived famous Black Hawk Down attack in Somalia and, more recently, three rounds of surgery for testicular cancer, dies in Iraq in crash of Black Hawk helicopter near Falluja; Weaver, who relatives say insisted on serving in Iraq, dies with eight others while en route to medical checkup; photo; he was awarded Bronze Star in 1994 (M)

2004-01-11.title: In Post-U.S.S.R. Russia, Any Job Is a Good Job
2004-01-11.abstract: Conditions at Apatit fertilizer factory in Arctic town of Kirovsk, Russia, reflect disruptions caused by post-Soviet society's tumultuous encounter with capitalism; few of Apatit's 14,000 workers want to stay, but even fewer have better options; production at factory lags far behind levels during Soviet era; Apatit is owned by jailed tycoon Mikhail B Khodorkovsky, who is charged with defaulting on payment in his 1994 acquisition of company, among other things; Khodorkovsky's defense points to massive capital investment in Apatit; photo (M)

2004-01-11.title: U.S. Soldiers Kill 2 Members Of Iraqi Police
2004-01-11.abstract: American soldiers kill two Iraqi police officers and detain a third unidentified man in gun battle under confused circumstances in Kirkuk, where ethnic tensions have been rising between Kurds and local Arabs and Turkmen; Danish troops find dozens of buried mortar rounds that might contain blister gas, which Saddam Hussein has said he had destroyed; photo (M)

2004-01-12.title: Jaime de Piniés, 86, Spanish Envoy to U.N.
2004-01-12.abstract: Jaime de Pinies, who was member of Spain's delegation to United Nations for 29 years and presided over 40th General Assembly in 1985, dies at age 86 (S)

2004-01-12.title: Expectations Are Low for Progress at Americas Conference
2004-01-12.abstract: Pres Bush and 33 elected leaders of the Americas will meet in Monterrey, Mex, without common vision for future of Western Hemisphere; expectations for great progress are low and friction on important proposals are high (M)

2004-01-12.title: Rainer Hildebrandt, Museum Head, 89, At Berlin Crossing
2004-01-12.abstract: Dr Rainer Hildebrandt, human rights activist and director of Museum dedicated to those who fled from Russian zone in Berlin during Cold War, dies at age 89; photo (M)

2004-01-12.title: In the Center Of Baghdad, An Escape To America
2004-01-12.abstract: United States Army has converted former Iraqi Republican Guard officers' club in Baghdad into little American oasis for war-weary soldiers; it now serves as First Armored Div's version of five-star hotel for as many as 100 soldiers at a time who are lucky enough to get three-day, two-night pass; more than 1,800 troops have cycled through since hotel opened in mid-Oct; amenities described; photo (M)

2004-01-12.title: An Eye for an Eye, a Tooth for a Tooth
2004-01-12.abstract: The United States, in its hunt for terrorists, is creating chaos in the world of travel. Now a provincial judge is organizing the resistance.

2004-01-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: 2 Get Life In Apartment Bombings
2004-01-13.abstract: Yusuf I Krymshamkhalov and Adam Dekkushev are convicted and sentenced to life in prison for 1999 bombing of three apartment buildings, in which 243 people were killed; critics of trial contend Russian secret service was involved in bombings, which took place in Moscow and Volgodonsk (S)

2004-01-13.title: Iran Leader Delays Ruling On Exclusion Of Reformists
2004-01-13.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme religious leader, says he will not intervene for moment in gathering confrontation between two main political camps in Iran--hard-liners and reformists--after review panel that he controls bars hundreds of reformist candidates from running in parliamentary elections next month; he meets with governors from around country who threaten to resign en masse if exclusions stand; he urges them to avoid tension; photo (M)

2004-01-13.title: Pakistani Judge Releases Pair Of Journalists
2004-01-13.abstract: Two French journalists sentenced to six months in jail for traveling to Pakistani city of Quetta without government's permission are freed on appeal; Pakistani journalist working with them is missing and believed to be in government detention; Pakistani officials had accused journalists of staging film about resurgent Taliban in Quetta, but never formally charged them in relation to those accusations; under policy begun last year, Pakistan's Interior Ministry issues journalists restricted visas that bar them from traveling to Quetta, Peshawar and certain other spots without government permission (M)

2004-01-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Sweden: Murder Charge In Minister's Stabbing
2004-01-13.abstract: Swedish prosecutors charge Mijailo Mijailovic with murder of Foreign Minister Anna Lindh, who was stabbed last September; Mijailovic has confessed, but holds stabbing was not politically motivated (S)

2004-01-13.title: British Doctor Who Killed 215 Hangs Himself
2004-01-13.abstract: Harold Shipman, the suburban doctor who won his patients trust before killing at least 215 of them, committed suicide in his prison cell today.

2004-01-14.title: Bush Meets Skepticism on Free Trade at Americas Conference
2004-01-14.abstract: Pres Bush gets skeptical response from other Western Hemisphere leaders when he argues at meeting in Monterrey, Mexico, that free trade is key to lifting tens of millions in hemisphere out of poverty; final declaration does not include 2005 deadline sought by Bush for Free Trade Area of the Americas, nor does it ban 'corrupt' governments from future meetings or adopt Bush's thinking that trade is best, most certain path to prosperity; photo (M)

2004-01-14.title: Army Copter Downed West of Baghdad in Hotbed of Anti-U.S. Sentiment
2004-01-14.abstract: Iraq staggers through another day of violence and instability; guerrillas shoot down Apache helicopter in Habbaniya; two Iraqi civilians are killed by American soldiers who open fire during anti-Americans protest in Falluja; four relatives of Izzat Ibrahim al-Douri, Saddam Hussein's former vice president and the most wanted fugitive in Iraq, are arrested in Samarra; photos (M)

2004-01-14.title: Killer of Swedens Foreign Minister Tells of Hearing Voices
2004-01-14.abstract: The confessed killer of Anna Lindh, Swedens foreign minister, denied today that he had intended to kill her but said he could not ignore voices in his head.

2004-01-14.title: World Briefing | Africa: Polio Spread From Nigeria, W.H.O. Confirms
2004-01-14.abstract: World Health Organization confirms that two new cases of polio in Benin and Cameroon were spread from Nigeria; is concerned that Muslim leaders have suspended vaccination in parts of northern Nigeria (S)

2004-01-14.title: Hurray! A Constitution! (Tell It to the Warlords)
2004-01-14.abstract: Adoption of Constitution by Afghan loya jirga does not end lawlessness and human rights abuses that that prevail in provinces outside Kabul, especially in remote areas where self-appointed commanders reign; Afghan Human Rights Commission has recorded 1,700 complaints of violations from around country, including 225 accusations of murder involving commanders or other officials; photo (M)

2004-01-15.title: Kennedy Says War in Iraq Was Choice, Not Necessity
2004-01-15.abstract: Sen Edward Kennedy says Pres Bush has broken bond of trust with Americans, marketing Iraq like 'political product' to elect Republicans and reaping 'poison fruit of our misguided and arrogant foreign policy'; speech to Center for American Progress charges administration used fear after Sept 11 attacks to justify war of choice; singles out Vice Pres Cheney, Sec Donald Rumsfeld and Deputy Sec Paul Wolfowitz as 'axis of war' (M)

2004-01-15.title: South Korea Aide Resigns in Rift Over U.S. Ties
2004-01-15.abstract: South Koreas foreign minister resigned today, accepting responsibility for a rift between pro-U.S. officials at his ministry and members of President Roh Moo Hyuns staff.

2004-01-15.title: Iraqi Team Disables Bombs With a Snip and a Prayer
2004-01-15.abstract: Baghdad police bomb squad, which existed under Saddam Hussein but was far less active, defuses many more bombs each day than actually explode; photos (M)

2004-01-15.title: A Suicide Bomber Kills at Least 2 Iraqis at a Police Station Northeast of Baghdad
2004-01-15.abstract: Car bomb explodes outside police headquarters in Baquba, Iraq, killing driver and at least two bystanders and wounding 26 others, mostly policemen; Gen Mark Kimmitt announces capture of Khamis Sirhan al-Muhammad, 54 on US list of 55 most-wanted former officials; two Pakistanis and one Turk traveling with supply convoy are killed near Tikrit; 62 Bulgarian soldiers reportedly have left 480-member peacekeeping unit bound for Iraq; Francis J Ricciardone is named State Department director of policy on Iraq (M)

2004-01-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Rains Tilt U.S.-Flagged Cruise Ship
2004-01-15.abstract: Pride of America, cruise ship, takes on water and tilts 15 degrees starboard during North Sea storm; first new oceangoing ship to fly United States flag in 50 years is still in final construction stage in Bremmerhaven (Germany) and accident is under investigation (S)

2004-01-16.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-16.abstract: RUSSIA: Inquiry Into Pneumonia In Army; BRITAIN: Here Lies, There Goes;.

2004-01-16.title: U.S. JOINS IRAQIS TO SEEK U.N. ROLE IN INTERIM RULE
2004-01-16.abstract: Bush administration, hoping to rescue its troubled plan to restore sovereignty to Iraq, is joining Iraqi leaders to press United Nations to play role in choosing interim government in Baghdad; American administrator L Paul Bremer III and Iraqi delegation led by Adnan Pachachi, chairman of Iraqi Governing Council, will make urgent appeal for greater UN involvement; tens of thousands of demonstrators in Basra put pressure on US to support calls by leading Shiite cleric Grand Ayatollah Ali Al-Sistani for direct elections; new move at UN involves yet another change in strategy for administration under pressure from shifting events in Iraq; administration has had ambivalent attitude toward UN from very start of planning war to oust Saddam Hussein; it is also considering reversing itself and allowing businesses in countries that opposed war, including France, Germany and Russia, to bid on contracts to rebuild Iraq; photos (M)

2004-01-16.title: Fresh SARS Cases in China Called Milder
2004-01-16.abstract: World Health Orgn official says three cases of SARS that have been identified in Guangzhou, China, so far this winter appear to be milder strain of disease, and public health system seems well prepared to prevent larger outbreak (S)

2004-01-16.title: Rome Journal; Is Berlusconi Remaking Himself With Eyes to the Future?
2004-01-16.abstract: Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi of Italy has virtually disappeared from public view in last month; that and his reputation for cosmetic enhancement have prompted reports that he has undergone plastic surgery; he and his party face several political tests in coming months, and associate says he 'needs to look good'; photo (M)

2004-01-16.title: Political Barbs Welcome An Iranian Visiting France
2004-01-16.abstract: Hassan Rowhani, head of Iran's National Security Council, meets in Paris with French Foreign Min Dominque de Villepin, and two men clash over coming Iranian parliamentary elections and human rights; de Villepin uses blunt language unusual in diplomacy, demanding that Iran's elections next month be free and fair and that people arrested for their political beliefs be released; Rowhani strongly defends Iran's electoral process and rejects what he calls 'interference of any country' in Iran's internal affairs; Rowhani negotiated accord last Oct with de Villepin and his British and German counterparts that commits Iran to agree to more intrusive international inspections of its nuclear sites by International Atomic Energy Agency and suspension of its activities to enrich uranium; Rowhani insists his country is respecting its agreements, despite strong suspicions that Iran continues to assemble centrifuges that can be used to enrich uranium (M)

2004-01-17.title: Dr. Norman Heatley, 92, Dies; Pioneer in Penicillin Supply
2004-01-17.abstract: Penicillin developer Dr Norman Heatley dies at age 92 (M)

2004-01-17.title: Russia Sees Iraqi Debt Relief As Link to Oil, U.S. Aides Say
2004-01-17.abstract: Bush administration says Russia is hoping to leverage its plans for debt relief for Iraq to win revival of oil development projects signed under Saddam Hussein and suspended by occupation authorities (M)

2004-01-17.title: Names of the Dead
2004-01-17.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of one American service member in Iraq (S)

2004-01-17.title: U.S. Officials Try to Trace Illegal Sale of Nuclear Technology
2004-01-17.abstract: US law enforcement officials are trying to determine whether Pakistani government is involved in plot by South African businessman Asher Karni to export trigger devices that could be used for nuclear weapons; Karni, Israeli who lives in South Africa, was arrested in Denver in early Jan on charges that he illegally exported devices to Pakistan without license; is accused of ordering 200 trigger devices from PerkinElmer Optoelectronics of Salem, Mass (M)

2004-01-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: TV Host Out After Arab Remarks
2004-01-17.abstract: BBC talk show host Robert Kilroy-Silk, who has been on air for 17 years, quits because of uproar surrounding anti-Arab comments he made in Sunday Express newspaper column; complaints were lodged by Muslim Council of Britain among other groups; photo (S)

2004-01-18.title: Bordello Business Unsettled After Spanish Court Ruling
2004-01-18.abstract: A court in the city of Seville has ruled that the owner of a so-called alternative club must make social security payments for 12 women who worked there as prostitutes.

2004-01-18.title: Deadly Blast in Baghdad
2004-01-18.abstract: A large bomb exploded at the main gate of the American headquarters in Baghdad, killing and wounding people gathered nearby. Page 12.

2004-01-18.title: Flight to U.S. Lands in Ireland After Bomb Scare
2004-01-18.abstract: Irish police evacuated all 147 people on board within 10 minutes and began interviewing passengers about a note saying a bomb was on board.

2004-01-18.title: Prince Alfonso Hohenlohe-Langenburg, Who Built Resorts, Dies at 79
2004-01-18.abstract: Prince Alfonso Hohenlohe-Langenburg turned the Spanish fishing village of Marbella into a glittery jet-set playground.

2004-01-18.title: Blast in Baghdad Kills at Least 20 Outside U.S. Post
2004-01-18.abstract: The truck bomb, which exploded at the main gate of the American occupation headquarters, killed at least 20 people and wounded at least 60 others.

2004-01-19.title: Kapawi Journal; Ecuador Indians Fend Off Oil Companies With Tourism
2004-01-19.abstract: Achuar Indians, dominant tribe in Ecuadorean Amazon, help run Kapawi Ecolodge and Reserve, offering ecology-friendly getaways and jungle tours; for Achuar people, Kapawi is increasingly important symbol of their independence as they try to resist oil companies that want to drill there; their idea is to demonstrate options to oil exploration; photos; map (M)

2004-01-19.title: Sharon Hints Israel Might Alter Route of West Bank Barrier, but Not to Meet Outside Demands
2004-01-19.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon says Israel might alter route of its separation barrier in West Bank, acknowledging that barrier has caused difficulties for ordinary Palestinians; says that in making alterations, his government would not be swayed by demands made by Palestinians, United Nations or International Court of Justice in The Hague; Israel says barrier is intended as security measure against suicide bombers and other Palestinian attacks; Palestinians say it amounts to confiscation of West Bank land they are demanding for future state; photo (M)

2004-01-19.title: Prince Alfonso Hohenlohe-Langenburg, 79; Built Resorts
2004-01-19.abstract: Resort developer Prince Alfonso Hohenlohe-Lnagenburg dies at age 79; photo (M)

2004-01-19.title: A Suicide Bombing Spreads Anguish Through the Working-Class Families of Baghdad
2004-01-19.abstract: Dozens of people wounded in latest suicide bombing in Baghdad pour into hospitals, while relatives of those missing visit yard of city morgue; Iraqi insurgents are mounting their most spectacular attacks against Iraqis themselves; working for Americans makes Iraqi a traitor in eyes of insurgents, and viable target; in Iraq's ravaged economy, Americans are among few sources of steady pay; photo (M)

2004-01-19.title: U.N. Pressed to Send Team to Iraq to Aid Political Transition
2004-01-19.abstract: United Nations Secretary General Kofi Annan indicated that he would like to help but that further meetings were necessary.

2004-01-20.title: Next Target in the French Headgear Debate: The Bandanna
2004-01-20.abstract: The proposed French law prohibiting the wearing of religious symbols in public schools was expanded today to include bandannas worn by girls.

2004-01-20.title: G.I.'s Headed for Iraq Train For Peace as Well as War
2004-01-20.abstract: Lt Gen Thomas F Metz, who will soon take command of day-to-day military operations in Iraq, orders training for Army's III Corps for mission that goes far beyond usual tabletop war games and field exercises; headquarters staff of III Corps, at Fort Hood, Tex, drills with scenarios cast well into June for weather and religious holidays and local politics; Metz has assigned his commanders and senior noncommissioned officers reading list on Islam; knows his troops will be engaged in everything from firefights to fixing sewers; photo (M)

2004-01-20.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-20.abstract: NORWAY: 2 Dead, 16 Missing As Freighter Capsizes; BRITAIN: Cases Of Infant Death To Be Reopened; IRELAND: Arrest In Plane Hoax; NORTHERN IRELAND: Power-Sharing Review; SWEDEN: Suspect In MinisterS Death Must Undergo Tests; FINLAND: Rigor Mortis On The Job;.

2004-01-20.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Arrest In Plane Hoax
2004-01-20.abstract: Irish police arrest 19-year-old passenger on Delta airlines flight from Frankfurt (Germany) to Atlanta after bomb scare forces emergency landing in Shannon (Ireland); flight continues on to destination (S)

2004-01-20.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Cases Of Infant Death To Be Reopened
2004-01-20.abstract: British Court of Appeal states it plans to halt all prosecution of deaths that could have been caused by sudden infant death syndrome and will review past convictions; 258 British adults have been convicted of killing child under age 2 and more than 50 are still in prison (S)

2004-01-21.title: Top Iranian Council Reinstates 200 Parliamentary Candidates
2004-01-21.abstract: Iran's Guardian Council, which touched off storm by rejecting 3,600 candidates for parliament, backs off somewhat, reinstating 200 and announcing plans to approve more (S)

2004-01-21.title: Iraqis Again Seek Elections and a Local Trial for Hussein
2004-01-21.abstract: Thousands of Iraqis demonstrate again in four major cities, demanding that US hand over Saddam Hussein to stand trial and renewing calls for direct elections; photo (S)

2004-01-21.title: U.S. Says Only Taliban Died in Raid
2004-01-21.abstract: US military says raid in southern Afghanistan killed only five Taliban fighters, not 11 civilians as Afghan officials reported (S)

2004-01-21.title: Putin Eager to Show Russian Gains In Meeting With World Bank Head
2004-01-21.abstract: Pres Vladimir Putin tells visiting World Bank head James D Wolfensohn that Russia wants to use its higher profile as Bank member to illustrate gains in economic and fiscal situation (S)

2004-01-21.title: Colombia's Landed Gentry: Coca Lords and Other Bullies
2004-01-21.abstract: Rightwing paramilitaries are taking over Colombia's most fertile land, forcing farmers off or intimidating them into selling at low prices, even as government tries to wrest control of historically lawless country; photo; paramilitary leaders are negotiating with government while quietly laundering drug money by taking huge tracts from poor farmers and even big landowners (M)

2004-01-22.title: Dead Falcon in Hong Kong Found to Be Infected With Bird Flu
2004-01-22.abstract: Agricultural officials in Hong Kong report that dead peregrine falcon found near two chicken farms there was infected with avian influenza virus, in first sign that disease may be present in China; it is spreading in chicken flocks in Vietnam, South Korea and Japan; World Health Orgn officials describe scale of spread as 'unprecedented' and warn that disease could evolve to spread from person to person, and not just from birds to people; Thailand and Laos have also reported large scale deaths of chickens in last several days and Thai officials say they are testing three people for possible bird flu; WHO has confirmed five cases in people in Vietnam, all of whom have died (M)

2004-01-22.title: World Briefing: Middle East
2004-01-22.abstract: LIBYA: U.S. Lawmakers Plan Trip; EGYPT: Belly Ban Continues;.

2004-01-22.title: Iran's Leader Mixes Hope With Defiance In Davos Talk
2004-01-22.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami of Iran offers mixed message of hope and defiance to this year's World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland; says he hopes Iran's election will be free and competitive, but he leaves open question of his own future; he threatened to resign after country's conservative religious leaders disqualified 3,600 candidates for parliamentary elections; he rejects Bush administation's allegations that Iran is seeking nuclear weapons; notes his government allowed United Nations inspectors to tour Iran's nuclear facilities; also dismisses suggestion that he meet with Vice Pres Dick Cheney, who will deliver speech in Davos well after Khatami is supposed to return to Tehran; Khatami has threatened to resign before in clashes between his reform-minded government and Guardian Council (M)

2004-01-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Sperm Donors To Lose Anonymity
2004-01-22.abstract: British government plans to eliminate anonymity for those who donate eggs and sperm after April 2005; plan needs Parliamentary approval (S)

2004-01-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Army Chief Quits
2004-01-22.abstract: German army head Lt Gen Gert Gudera asks for early retirement; will be replaced by Hans-Otto Budde (S)

2004-01-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Euro Counterfeits On The Rise
2004-01-23.abstract: European Commission reports sharp rise in number of Euro coin counterfiets in 2003; national banks detected only 2,339 fake coins in 2002, compared to 26,191 coins discovered in 2003 (S)

2004-01-23.title: World Briefing: Africa
2004-01-23.abstract: RWANDA: Ex-Minister Gets Life In 1994 Genocide; IVORY COAST: Policeman Guilty Of Murder;.

2004-01-23.title: U.S. and Saudis Act to Freeze Charity's Assets
2004-01-23.abstract: Bush administration says it has teamed up with Saudi Arabia to freeze assets of four branches of major Islamic charitable foundation that they accuse of providing financial support to terrorist groups; single out branches in Kenya, Tanzania, Pakistan and Indonesia of Al-Haramain Charitable Foundation; Saudi officials say they have taken many steps to rein in charities that funnel money to Islamic terrorist groups, and they have ordered Al-Haramain to close all overseas branches; say they are not prepared to act against parent organization, which raises $50 million a year and finances legitimate charitable projects (M)

2004-01-23.title: Brazil's Soaring Space-Age Ambitions Are Shy of Cash and Sapped by Calamity
2004-01-23.abstract: Second of three articles on expanding space race in developing world focuses on Brazil's unfulfilled space-age ambitions; country has tried three times to launch satellite on its own rocket; in most recent failure, in Aug, satellite rocket exploded on its launching pad, killing 21 scientists and technicians; that catastrophe provides vivid warning to other developing nations tempted to reach for grand ambitions in space on budgets already stretched thin by earthly needs; critics call such space programs vanity projects for poor nations still struggling to feed themselves; Brazilian officials, like those from India to China, describe their steadily expanding space effort as commercial and strategic necessity, as well as matter of national prestige; photos; map (L)

2004-01-23.title: Sharon Is Defiant in Face of Pressure Over Bribery Inquiry
2004-01-23.abstract: Two Israeli cabinet ministers say Prime Min Ariel Sharon would have to resign if bribery investigation eventually leads to his indictment; indictment against developer David Appel does not lay out evidence that Sharon knowingly took bribe; Sharon's predecessors as prime minister Ehud Barak and Benjamin Netanyahu served under threat of indictments that never materialized, but they were distracted and sapped politically by long investigation; Sharon brushes off possibility of his indictment; public opinion polls suggest his personal credibility is shaky, and jockeying has begun within his Likud party to eventually succeed him; photo of Palestinian house in Nablus blown up by Israeli Army during standoff with wanted Palestinian militant, who is thought to have escaped (M)

2004-01-24.title: Pakistan Chief Says It Appears Scientists Sold Nuclear Data
2004-01-24.abstract: Pakistan Pres Pervez Musharraf says scientists from his country appear to have sold nuclear designs to other nations; denies that government knew of sales; diplomats and US officials say documents recently obtained from Libya and Iran relating to their nuclear activities undercut years of Pakistani denials; believe that Pakistan received missile parts from North Korea in return for nuclear aid; International Atomic Energy Agency dir gen Mohamed ElBaradei comments; Musharraf photo (M)

2004-01-24.title: Thousands of Refugees Reported to Be Fleeing Sudan for Chad
2004-01-24.abstract: United Nations refugee agency is investigating reports that 18,000 Sudanese refugees, fleeing attacks on their villages, have crossed over into Chad; latest influx, adding to 95,000 Sudanese refugees already camped out near Chad-Sudan border, suggests that fighting in Sudanese region of Darfur continues to intensify, even as peace talks seek to end Sudan's other war, which has been going on for 20 years, between government in Khartoum and rebels in country's south; map (M)

2004-01-24.title: India's Lofty Ambitions in Space Meet Earthly Realities
2004-01-24.abstract: Last article in series The Next Space Race; India's national space program bills itself as thrifty space exploration for common man; India, with budget of only $450 million a year, has 13 satellites in orbit; it produces some of world's best remote imaging satellites and is planning to send satellite to moon by 2007 or 2008; only 6 of India's 37 satellite launchings have failed; engineers say they are using space technology to improve lives of tens of millions of Indians; say by using satellite imagery, they have helped 175 people in Majhgawan Karan reclaim 40 acres of barren land in area long haunted by hunger; photos (M)

2004-01-24.title: IRAQ ILLICIT ARMS GONE BEFORE WAR, INSPECTOR STATES
2004-01-24.abstract: David Kay, who led US effort to find banned weapons in Iraq, says after stepping down from his post that he has concluded that Iraq had no stockpiles of chemical and biological weapons at start of war; says he thinks Iraq had illicit weapons at end of 1991 Persian Gulf war, but that United Nations inspections and Iraq's own decisions 'got rid of them'; CIA Dir George J Tenet names Charles A Duelfer to replace Kay; Duelfer photo (M)

2004-01-24.title: Cross-cultural training : How much difference does it really make?
2004-01-24.abstract: VIENNA—  Before moving abroad, Alecia Myers and her husband Ken drove to Chicago to attend a daylong cross-cultural training seminar designed to teach them how to understand and relate to people from foreign cultures. There they learned about high context and low context cultures, sub-cultures, and co-cultures, the seven dimensions of culture, and increasing intercultural business competency. One year later, happily settled in Austria, Alecia looks back favorably on her cross-cultural training and tries to remember what she learned.

2004-01-25.title: Pakistan TV: A New Look At the News
2004-01-25.abstract: Geo TV, Pakistani cable station transmitting from Dubai, is example of private television stations that are gaining favor over state-run media across South Asia; Pakistani Pres Pervez Musharraf reversed ban on private television stations in 2002, in part because many Pakistanis turned to Indian cable stations for news rather than state-run Pakistan Television; ban on Indian programming remains in place; private media ownership allows greater freedom in material transmitted; photos (M)

2004-01-25.title: Expulsion of Israeli Soldiers Father Is Suspended
2004-01-25.abstract: Israels high court today suspended for 30 days the states efforts to expel the Palestinian father of an Israeli soldier, pending a hearing on granting him the right to remain in Israel.

2004-01-25.title: Israel Agrees To Free Arabs In a Swap With Militants
2004-01-25.abstract: Israel and Hezbollah agree to prisoner exchange brokered by Germany; Israel will return more than 400 prisoners of various nationalities captured during its occupation of southern Lebanon, as well as remains of fighters killed there; Hezbollah will free kidnapped Israeli businessman Elhanan Tannenbaum and hand over remains of three dead Israelis; exchange has produced anguished debate in Israel, with government, public and families of missing Israelis expressing mixed feelings (M)

2004-01-25.title: 5 G.I.'s Are Killed in a Wave of Violence
2004-01-25.abstract: Five American soldiers and four Iraqis are killed and dozens of Iraqis are wounded in series of attacks by Iraqi insurgents; US and Iraqi forces continue to conduct series of aggressive raids, including 52 on Jan 23 alone, bringing in prisoners, bomb-making materials and documents associated with intelligence services of former regime; Brig Gen Mark Kimmitt says attacks are effort to delay advent of democracy in Iraq; photo; map (M)

2004-01-25.title: Italian Leader And His Party Face Questions At Anniversary
2004-01-25.abstract: Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi's Forza Italia party celebrates its 10th anniversary; news media's focus on Berlusconi amid rumors that he has undergone cosmetic surgery highlights political analysts' doubts about whether party has its own identity and future or is entirely dependent on Berlusconi for its face, voice and vigor; Forza Italia holds more seats in parliament than any other party, but some analysts say it has no ideological underpinning or direction (M)

2004-01-26.title: Israeli Court Suspends Expulsion Of Soldier's Palestinian Father
2004-01-26.abstract: Israel's high court suspends for 30 days state's efforts to expel Adel Hussein, Palestinian father of an Israeli soldier, pending hearing on granting him right to remain in Israel; Hussein has been living illegally in Israel for years while seeking permission to remain legally; his ex-wife and son, Israeli Jews, have been lobbying on his behalf; family's circumstances are being widely covered in Israel, and Hussein's lawyer says public pressure is helping (M)

2004-01-26.title: Hezbollah Hails Trading Of Prisoners
2004-01-26.abstract: Hezbollah leader Sheik Hassan Nasrallah makes rare public appearance in Beirut, Lebanon, to trumpet as a victory prisoner-exchange deal with Israel, in which 430 Palestinians, Lebanese and other Arab prisoners will be swapped for kidnapped Israeli businessman and three Israeli soldiers kidnapped along Lebanese border in 2000; Nasrallah holds out possibility that not all soldiers are dead, as Israelis assume; complicated deal is sealed after months of painstaking negotiations with German mediator; some in Israel call deal lopsided and say it could encourage more kidnappings; Prime Min Ariel Sharon defends deal but says it was not easy decision; he emphasizes importance of second phase of deal: that Hezbollah will give 'significant, proven and clear information' about fate of Capt Ron Arad, Israeli Air Force navigator shot down in Lebanon in 1986 and captured by Lebanese guerrillas; United States considers Hezbollah one of most potent terrorist organizations; photo (M)

2004-01-26.title: Ex-Inspector Says C.I.A. Missed Disarray in Iraqi Arms Program
2004-01-26.abstract: David A Kay, recently resigned chief weapons inspector for CIA, says American intelligence agencies failed to recognize that Iraq had all but abandoned its efforts to produce large quantities of chemical or biological weapons after first Persian Gulf war in 1991; says they did not detect that Iraq's unconventional weapons programs were in state of disarray in recent years under increasingly erratic leadership of Saddam Hussein, and they did not know that Hussein insisted on self-directing projects that were not vetted by anyone else, allowing wily scientists to fake weapons programs and pocket money themselves; says Iraq did attempt to revive its efforts to develop nuclear weapons in 2000 and 2001, but never got as far toward making bomb as Iran and Libya did; says Baghdad was working to produce biological weapon using poison ricon until American invasion last Mar; says Tariq Aziz, former deputy prime minister, said after his capture that Hussein had become increasingly divorced from reality during last two years of his rule; Kay says CIA missed significance of chaos in Iraqi leadership; photo (L)

2004-01-26.title: The Mullah and Democracy
2004-01-26.abstract: To counter the threats made by Shiite leader Sistani, the Americans, at a loss, are now turning to the United Nations again. They want the UN to help bring peace to Iraq.

2004-01-26.title: OBITUARY : Helmut Newton, master of the perverse image
2004-01-26.abstract: Helmut Newton, the master photographer of sexual perversity and the founding father of androgynous imagery, died Friday at 83 in a way that he might have scripted in his narrative fashion photographs: from an accident in his Cadillac at Chateau Marmont, the star-studded Hollywood hotel. He was wintering there as usual, with his wife of 55 years, June, known as the photographer Alice Springs.

2004-01-27.title: White House Shows Less Certainty Now About Iraq's Arms
2004-01-27.abstract: White House backs away from assertions that Iraq had illegal weapons, saying administration wants to compare prewar intelligence assessments with what may be actually found; outgoing weapons inspector Dr David Kay's criticism of prewar intelligence increases pressure on CIA and intensifies debate on who shaped reports that Pres Bush used to justify invasion; White House stops asserting that weapons will eventually be found, although press secretary Scott McClellan says Bush was right to depose Saddam Hussein given his history; Kay reports scant evidence that Hussein had stockpiles of illicit weapons and says CIA under George Tenet was wrong in its contrary assessment (M)

2004-01-27.title: General Denies Letting Secrets Of A-Bomb Out Of Pakistan
2004-01-27.abstract: Gen Mirza Aslam Beg, former commander of Pakistan's army, says he never approved transfer of atomic information; senior nuclear scientists reportedly say Beg would have had to approve technology transfer to Iran; Beg says scientists should not be punished for selling nuclear technology and that Muslim countries should not give up pursuit of such weapons until India and Israel destroy arsenals; interview; photos; expresses deep antipathy to US (M)

2004-01-27.title: British Leader Narrowly Wins Key Vote
2004-01-27.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair today avoided a political humiliation that threatened to bring down his government.

2004-01-27.title: Olivier Guichard, 83, One of the Last Barons of Gaullism
2004-01-27.abstract: Olivier Guichard, guardian of France's Gaullist legacy and architect of its regional administration, dies at age 83; photo (M)

2004-01-27.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Constitution Is Law
2004-01-27.abstract: Afghan President Hamid Karzai, at Kabul ceremony, signs Afghanistan's new Constitution into law; document was approved by loya jirga, grand council, on January 4; former King Mohammad Zahir Shah attends signing ceremony; photo (S)

2004-01-28.title: Blair Narrowly Prevails in a Vote to Raise Tuition Fees
2004-01-28.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair narrowly defeats revolt in his Labor Party, winning passage, 316-311, of bill to revamp Britain's higher education system and avoiding political humiliation that threatened to bring down his government; bill will require university students, who now make only nominal tuition payments, to begin paying up to $5,500 a year starting in 2006; existing tuition, which is means-tested and paid by fewer than half of students, was put in place by Blair in 1998 and has been object of protests; opposition Conservatives have called for complete tuition rollback (M)

2004-01-28.title: America Tugs at French-Accented Lands: It's Not Peanuts
2004-01-28.abstract: Senegal, long dominated by France, is marching headlong into United States camp now that war on terrorism has spurred US interest in Africa; responds to number of factors, including drop in French aid; France remains biggest donor and trade partner, but Senegal's relations with US have already strained its ties with France; map; photo (M)

2004-01-28.title: Powell Seeks to Reassure Russians on New Troops
2004-01-28.abstract: State Sec Colin L Powell seeks to assure Russians that United States has no intention of encircling or threatening Russia despite American plans to redeploy some troops to former Soviet bloc nations in Eastern Europe, Moscow radio interview; says American forces in Europe are falling from over 300,000 to under 100,000; notes Pres Bush weighs putting 'temporary facilities' in Eastern Europe for training or use as air bases; says he got assurances from Pres Vladimir V Putin that charges against Mikhail B Khodorkovsky will be fully explained and that his trial will be open; says US has no intention of expelling Russia from Group of 8 industrial nations because of case; cites need to exercise restraint in war with Chechen separatists in accordance with human rights norms; dismisses idea that US-Russia ties are chilling or that Russian democracy is in danger (M)

2004-01-28.title: Russia Issues International Wanted List in Oil Case
2004-01-28.abstract: Russia places 10 Yukos Oil shareholders or employees on international wanted lists, including some senior executives who have sought asylum in Israel; move suggests prosecution of Yukos is extending far beyond former chief executive Mikhail B Kodorkovsky and his partner, Platon Lebedev, who are both in jail; Russia names three of those sought, Leonid B Nevzlin, Mikhail B Brudno and Vladimir M Dubov, all of whom are in Israel; Moscow court rejects Khodorkovsky's challenge of his Oct arrest (M)

2004-01-28.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Wave Of Killings
2004-01-28.abstract: At least 25 people have been killed in drug related violence across Mexico including federal drug agents and former Deputy State Attorney General (S)

2004-01-29.title: Second Top BBC Executive Quits; Blair Accepts Apology
2004-01-29.abstract: The BBCs director general resigned today, saying that he hoped his departure would staunch the damage from a devastating report.

2004-01-29.title: Report on Iraq Case Clears Blair and Faults BBC
2004-01-29.abstract: Long-awaited report by Lord Hutton, senior British judge, clears Prime Min Tony Blair and his government of any deliberate attempt to deceive public on threat from Iraq mentioned in Sept 2002 intelligence dossier; castigates BBC for sloppy, inaccurate reporting and 'defective' editorial supervision in asserting that Blair and aides exaggerated case for war; Lord Hutton reads conclusions from bench in Royal Courts of Justice; photos; BBC chairman Gavyn Davies later resigns in one of worst debacles in famous network's history; findings are personal vindication for Blair, beleaguered by rebellion within Labor Party; BBC editor-in-chief Greg Dyke issues recorded statement saying broadcaster Andrew Gilligan has already admitted that 'certain key allegations' were wrong; defends right to reveal concerns of Dr David Kelly, weapons scientist who was Gilligan's source; Kelly's suicide last July set off uproar; Blair, in Parliament, indignantly demands apology (M)

2004-01-29.title: Explosion in Afghanistan Kills 7 U.S. Soldiers and Injures 3
2004-01-29.abstract: The cause of one of the single most-deadly incidents involving U.S. forces in Afghanistan since the war remained unclear.

2004-01-29.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Cabinet Backs Rule Against Scarves
2004-01-29.abstract: French cabinet approves draft law prohibiting wearing of religious symbols, including Islamic head scarves, in public schools; bill, supported by President Jacques Chirac, must be approved by National Assembly to become law (S)

2004-01-29.title: World Briefing: Asia
2004-01-29.abstract: PHILIPPINES: Executions Stayed; CHINA: Sect Sues Minister;.

2004-01-30.title: World Briefing | Africa: Sudan: Plane Bombs Chad Side Of Border
2004-01-30.abstract: United Nations official reports that Sudanese plane bombed border town in Chad as part of effort to quell year-long western Darfur area insurgency; 2 civilians and 15 others are killed (S)

2004-01-30.title: French Court Finds Ex-Minister Guilty of Illegal Party Funding
2004-01-30.abstract: In a blow to President Jacques Chirac, the former prime minister Alain Juppé was found guilty today of illegal party financing.

2004-01-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: Taiwan: Rebuke To Chirac
2004-01-30.abstract: Taiwan, protesting French President Jacques Chirac's criticism of island's planned missile defense referendum, will break off high-level government exchanges with France (S)

2004-01-30.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-30.abstract: GERMANY: 9/11 Prisoner Seeks Retrial; BRITAIN: Marijuana Laws Relaxed; BRITAIN: Couple Out Over Puppy Farming;.

2004-01-30.title: Turkey, Seeking U.S. Pledge on the Kurds' Role in the New Iraq, Finds Mixed Message
2004-01-30.abstract: Sec of State Colin L Powell, meeting with Turkish Prime Min Recep Tayyip Erdogan in Washington, offers assurances that Kurds in a newly soverign Iraq will have to relinquish their control of oil resources in north; Turkish officials say they remain concerned that United States may still yield to Kurdish demands for autonomous state; photo (M)

2004-01-31.title: NEW IRAQ AGENCY TO HUNT REBELS
2004-01-31.abstract: Iraqi authorities, with help of US intelligence agencies, are creating intelligence service that will focus on rooting out guerrilla fighters, especially those from outside country; service will employ some former agents of Saddam Hussein's security apparatus and will probably receive financing from US; it will employ 500 to 2,000 people, and is expected to be formed before Bush administration transfers sovereignty to Iraqi government on June 30; CIA is taking lead in helping put together new service; photo shows partly destroyed headquarters of Saddam Hussein's secret police (M)

2004-01-31.title: Reporter at Center of Dispute Over Iraq Resigns From BBC
2004-01-31.abstract: Andrew Gilligan, reporter at center of dispute between British Broadcasting Corp and British government over whether government had exaggerated case for war against Iraq, resigns from BBC; follows BBC chmn Gavyn Davies and dir gen Greg Dyke, who left their posts after scathing official report that accused Gilligan of sloppy reporting and his employers of host of journalistic and management failures (M)

2004-01-31.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Court Backs Spanking
2004-01-31.abstract: Canadian Supreme Court rules that parents can spank their children but decision limits use of punishment to children over age 2 and under age 12 and charges parents to control degree of force applied (S)

2004-01-31.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Prosecutor For Unexplained Killings
2004-01-31.abstract: Mexican Attorney General names special prosecutor to investigate unsolved murders of at least 300 women in Ciudad Juarez over past ten years (S)

2004-01-31.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-01-31.abstract: SERBIA: Close, But No Speaker Vote; GERMANY: 9/11 Suspect Called A Codes Expert; GERMANY: SchrÖDer To Visit White House; ROMANIA: Candidate Seeks A New Image; RUSSIA: U.S. Fails On Agreement For Weapons Raids; THE NETHERLANDS: Support For Arms To China; BELGIUM: Minister Chided For U.S. Criticism;.
